_meta.source: Windsor.ai Google Ads API — last 30 days
_meta.purpose: Feeds into Zakk Bot copy generation and optimization logic
_meta.note: Update this file weekly via slack_digest.py learning loop

top_performers.why_it_works: Horse rescue PMax with strong asset groups. Local equine rescue intent. Very specific audience — people actively searching for horses to adopt have high purchase intent.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Specificity wins. 'Meet a horse' and 'adopt a rescue horse' themes outperform generic animal copy. Visual assets are the differentiator in PMax.

top_performers.why_it_works: Highest CTR in entire portfolio at 20.4%. 'Get Involved' framing is broader than just adopt or donate — captures people at all stages of intent.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Inclusive CTAs ('Get Involved', 'Help Today') outperform narrow CTAs ('Donate Now') for awareness-stage audiences. Ziva's brand identity is strong enough to carry broad copy.

top_performers.why_it_works: Foster campaigns convert extremely well. People searching to foster are highly motivated — they want to help but aren't ready to adopt. Low barrier to entry = high conversion.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Foster copy should emphasize 'no long-term commitment', 'save a life temporarily', 'foster families needed urgently'. The emotional hook is saving a dog without the forever commitment.

top_performers.why_it_works: Blog/content landing pages convert at 2.5x despite lower CTR. Educational content builds trust before the ask. People arrive already understanding the mission.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Content-first approach works for complex missions (pet care assistance for seniors). Don't always lead with the ask — lead with education, then convert.

top_performers.why_it_works: Highest CTR in search campaigns across portfolio. Donation search with tight keyword focus on urgent dog rescue themes.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Donate search campaigns need urgency + specificity. 'Donate to save dogs' is weaker than 'LA dogs need rescue funds now'. Geographic specificity in donate copy drives CTR.

top_performers.why_it_works: Experience-based campaign. 'Meet the horse' is low-friction — people don't have to commit to adoption, just a visit. Once they visit, conversion to adoption/donation is high.
top_performers.copy_lesson: Low-barrier entry campaigns (visit, meet, learn) can outperform direct ask campaigns. Build the relationship first.

high_ctr_headline_themes: Geographic specificity: 'LA Dogs Need You Now', 'Help Arizona Rescue Dogs'
Urgency with numbers: '12 Dogs Need Homes This Week', '8 Urgent Cases'
Foster-specific: 'Foster One Dog', 'Be a Temporary Hero', 'Foster Needed Now'
Question format: 'Could You Save a Dog?', 'Ready to Change a Life?'
Low-commitment framing: 'Meet a Rescue Horse', 'Visit Our Ranch', 'Get Involved'
Mission transparency: '100% Goes to Animal Care', 'Your Gift Saves Lives Directly'
Short power: 'Adopt Today', 'Save a Dog', 'Help Now', 'Foster Now'
high_converting_description_themes: Specific impact: 'Your $25 covers one vet visit for a dog in our care'
Urgency + relief: 'Shelter is full. Your foster home could save a dog this week.'
Social proof: '347 dogs rescued this year with the help of supporters like you'
Educational lead-in: 'Millions of pets need care — here is how you can help today'
Low barrier: 'Fostering is temporary — the love lasts forever. Apply in 5 minutes.'

org_model_copy_rules.ALWAYS check org_model before writing co: Foster network copy should NEVER say 'visit us' or 'come meet'. Location-based copy should ALWAYS include city/county.
org_model_copy_rules.Horse rescue is not dog rescue: Equine copy needs ranch/sanctuary language, wider geo radius, sponsorship angle. Never use generic 'adopt a dog' templates.
org_model_copy_rules.Foster-first vs adopt-first: Foster network orgs need foster recruitment as primary CTA, adoption as secondary. Location-based shelters can lead with adoption.
org_model_copy_rules.Urgency calibration: Foster networks can always use urgency (animals in shelters). Location-based orgs should only use urgency when genuinely warranted.

Adopt.copy_direction: Specificity + local signal + low barrier. Show the animal's face in PMax assets. Use breed or species-specific keywords.
Adopt.landing_page_must_have: Individual animal profiles with photo, name, and story. Apply button above fold.

Donate.best_performer: Pup Profile Search-Donate (20.7% CTR, 81% conv rate)
Donate.copy_direction: Urgency + impact + geographic specificity. Lead with what the donation does, not what the org needs.
Donate.landing_page_must_have: Suggested donation amounts with impact statements. Single-step form. No redirect to third-party processor if possible.

Foster.copy_direction: Remove fear of commitment. 'Temporary' and 'saves a life' are the two most important concepts.
Foster.landing_page_must_have: What fostering involves (time, cost, support), success stories from foster families, simple form.

Get Involved.copy_direction: Broad inclusive language captures awareness-stage audiences. Good top-of-funnel campaign to warm up audiences for retargeting.
Get Involved.landing_page_must_have: Multiple ways to help (donate, foster, volunteer, share). Not just one CTA.

Volunteer.best_performer: Red Clay Ranch P Max Volunteer (lowest CPA issue)
Volunteer.copy_direction: Volunteer campaigns consistently underperform across portfolio. Conversion tracking may be issue. Focus on event-based volunteer asks rather than general.
Volunteer.landing_page_must_have: Specific volunteer opportunities with dates, time commitment, and sign-up form.

pmax.verdict: PMax wins on conversion volume and CPA. Use for established campaigns with good asset libraries.
pmax.requirements: Need minimum 3 image sizes (square/landscape/portrait), logo, 5+ headlines, 5+ descriptions, YouTube video if available.

search.verdict: Search wins on CTR and intent precision. Use for high-intent keywords and new campaigns where you need control.
search.requirements: Tight ad groups (5-10 keywords max), responsive search ads with 15 headlines, exact and phrase match mix.

pmax_vs_search.recommendation: Lead with Search to validate keywords and copy, then scale winners with PMax once you have conversion data.

retargeting_opportunities.overview: Retargeting (RLSA and Display) is not currently active on any account. Huge missed opportunity given the warm audiences being built.

audiences_to_build.size_estimate: 600+ people from Donate PMax alone last 30 days
audiences_to_build.retargeting_message: You visited our donation page — these dogs are still waiting. $25 changes everything.
audiences_to_build.urgency: CRITICAL — $2,913 spend building audiences with zero follow-up

audiences_to_build.retargeting_message: You saw animals that need medical care. They're still waiting for your help.
audiences_to_build.urgency: HIGH — medical case visitors have high emotional intent

audiences_to_build.retargeting_message: The dogs you saw are running out of time. Can you help today?
audiences_to_build.urgency: HIGH — urgency campaigns need retargeting to close the loop

audiences_to_build.retargeting_message: You learned about our mission. Now help us continue it with a donation.
audiences_to_build.urgency: MEDIUM — top of funnel, retarget with lower-barrier ask

how_to_build_retargeting.step_1: GA4 must be connected and tracking site visitors
how_to_build_retargeting.step_2: Create GA4 audience: 'Visited donate page, did not complete donation' (requires GA4 events)
how_to_build_retargeting.step_3: Import GA4 audience into Google Ads via the linked accounts
how_to_build_retargeting.step_4: Create Display or Search campaign with RLSA targeting
how_to_build_retargeting.step_5: Set bid adjustment: +50% for retargeting audiences on existing campaigns
how_to_build_retargeting.step_6: Create separate dedicated retargeting campaign with specific copy

priority_3_complete.note: These accounts have minimal spend currently. Set up GA4 before scaling campaigns.

for_each_client: 1. Identify every page where a user completes a meaningful action
2. Map each page to a conversion event name
3. Install GA4 tag (via GTM or direct code snippet)
4. Create custom events for each conversion trigger
5. Mark events as conversions in GA4 admin
8. Wait 48 hours and verify conversions are firing in Google Ads
pages_to_track_for_every_client: Thank you / confirmation page after donation
Scroll depth 75% on key pages (engagement micro-conversion)

org_models._note: These distinctions drive fundamentally different copy, keywords, landing pages, and conversion events

location_based.description: Has a physical facility — shelter, ranch, museum, clinic. People can visit, volunteer in person, attend events.
copy_principles: Geographic targeting is critical — always include city/county in headlines
Drive visits and in-person engagement — 'Meet the horses', 'Visit our shelter', 'Come see them'
Local search intent is strong — 'animal shelter near me', 'horse rescue [city]'
Event-based campaigns perform well — open houses, adoption events, fundraiser galas
Volunteer campaigns are high-value — on-site volunteers build long-term donors
location_based.retargeting_strategy: Retarget visitors with specific animal profiles — 'This horse is still looking for a home'

foster_network.description: No physical facility — operates entirely through foster homes. Animals live with volunteer foster families until adopted. Model depends on continuous foster recruitment.
copy_principles: Foster recruitment is the #1 priority — without fosters, rescue cannot operate
Remove the fear of commitment — 'temporary', 'we handle vet costs', 'we provide support'
Urgency is always real — dogs in shelters are on deadlines
Geographic targeting still matters but broader — drive to action not visit
The 'hero' narrative works — you save a life, not just adopt a pet
Showcase individual animals with names, photos, stories — drives emotional connection
foster_specific_copy: Headline: 'Save a Life From Your Home'
Description: 'We cover vet costs. You provide love. Foster a rescue dog and save a life.'
Description: 'No shelter space means dogs die. Your foster home is the solution. Apply today.'
landing_page_must_have: Individual dog profiles with photos, names, breed, story
Clear foster FAQ — costs, time, support provided
Urgency indicators — 'X dogs need fosters this week'
foster_network.retargeting_strategy: Retarget dog profile viewers with that specific dog — 'Bella is still waiting for a foster home'

equine_rescue.description: Horse, donkey, mule rescue. Fundamentally different from dog/cat rescue — horses require land, facilities, specialized care. Higher sponsorship potential, different donor profile.
copy_principles: Horses have names and personalities — individual horse stories are extremely powerful
'Meet the horse' campaigns outperform generic rescue campaigns (Red Clay 426% conv rate)
Sponsorship model is strong — people sponsor a specific horse monthly
Equine-specific language: 'sanctuary', 'rescue ranch', 'rehabilitate', 'rehome'
Audience is horse enthusiasts — they understand horse care costs, respond to specifics
Geographic radius must be wider — people travel for horses (100-150 mile radius normal)
Event campaigns perform well — ranch tours, open houses, ride-along days
Photography of horses in landscapes is the most powerful asset
horse_specific_copy: Description: 'Each horse has a name and a story. Sponsor [Horse Name] for $50/month and help cover feed and vet care.'
Description: 'Visit our ranch to meet horses rescued from neglect and abuse. Tours available weekends.'
landing_page_must_have: Individual horse profiles with names, photos, breed, backstory
Sponsorship levels with specific impact ($25/month = hay, $50/month = vet care)
equine_rescue.retargeting_strategy: Retarget horse profile viewers with that specific horse — 'Spirit is still waiting for a sponsor'

dog_rescue.description: Dog-specific rescue. Most common type. High emotional engagement, strong urgency potential, broad audience.
copy_principles: Individual dog stories with names and photos drive the highest engagement
Breed-specific copy converts well — 'Golden Retriever Rescue', 'Lab Mix Needs Home'
Urgency is very real and very effective — shelter deadlines, overcrowding
The adopt vs buy distinction resonates strongly with this audience
Foster-to-adopt pathway should always be mentioned
Medical cases are powerful fundraising hooks
urgency_copy: Headline: '[X] Dogs Need Rescue This Week'
Description: '[Dog Name] is running out of time. Your adoption or foster application could save a life today.'

cat_rescue.description: Cat-specific or cat-inclusive rescue. Different search behavior than dogs — cat adopters search differently.
copy_principles: Cat adopters are often apartment dwellers — 'apartment friendly', 'independent', 'low maintenance' converts
Senior cats and bonded pairs are harder to place — make specific asks for these
TNR (trap-neuter-return) community cat programs have a different audience
Medical cats are strong fundraising hooks
cat_specific_copy: Description: 'Our cats are vet-checked, spayed/neutered, and ready to come home. Find your perfect match.'

wildlife_rescue.description: Wildlife, birds, marine animals. Very different from domestic animal rescue — no adoption, focus on release and habitat.
copy_principles: No adoption campaigns — focus on donate and sponsor
Education angle is powerful — awareness of threats, habitat loss
Specific species campaigns work well — 'Save the sea turtles'
Release milestones are powerful content — 'X animals released this year'
